                                                                                                              • SKILLOPT: Executive Strategy for Self-Evolving Agent Skills

                                                                                                              SKILLOPT：自演化智能体技能的执行策略

                                                                                                              Yifan Yang 1,∗,‡ Ziyang Gong 2,∗ Weiquan Huang 3,∗ Qihao Yang 2,∗ Ziwei Zhou 4,∗ Zisu Huang 4,∗ Yan Li 2 Xuemei Gao 1 Qi Dai 1 Bei Liu 1 Kai Qiu 1 Yuqing Yang 1 Dongdong Chen 1 Xue Yang 2,‡ Chong Luo 1 Yifan Yang 1,∗,‡ Ziyang Gong 2,∗ Weiquan Huang 3,∗ Qihao Yang 2,∗ Ziwei Zhou 4,∗ Zisu Huang 4,∗ Yan Li 2 Xuemei Gao 1 Qi Dai 1 Bei Liu 1 Kai Qiu 1 Yuqing Yang 1 Dongdong Chen 1 Xue Yang 2,‡ Chong Luo 1

                                                                                                              1 Microsoft 2 Shanghai Jiao Tong University 3 Tongji University 4 Fudan University 1 Microsoft 2 上海交通大学 3 同济大学 4 复旦大学

                                                                                                              Agent skills today are hand-crafted, generated one-shot, or evolved through loosely controlled self-revision - none of which behaves like a deep-learning optimizer for the skill, and none of which reliably improves over its starting point under feedback. We argue the skill should instead be trained as the external state of a frozen agent, with the same discipline that makes weight-space optimization reproducible. SKILLOPT is, to our knowledge, the first systematic controllable text-space optimizer for agent skills: a separate optimizer model turns scored rollouts into bounded add/delete/replace edits on a single skill document, and an edit is accepted only when it strictly improves a held-out validation score. A textual learning-rate budget, rejected-edit buffer, and epoch-wise slow/meta update make skill training stable while adding zero inference-time model calls at deployment. Across six benchmarks, seven target models, and three execution harnesses (direct chat, Codex, Claude Code), SKILLOPT is best or tied on all 52 evaluated (model, benchmark, harness) cells and beats every per-cell competitor among human, one-shot LLM, Trace2Skill, TextGrad, GEPA, and EvoSkill skills. On GPT-5.5 it lifts the average no-skill accuracy by +23.5 points in direct chat, by +24.8 inside the Codex agentic loop, and by +19.1 inside Claude Code. Transfer experiments further show that optimized skill artifacts retain value when moved across model scales, between Codex and Claude Code execution environments, and to a nearby math benchmark without further optimization. 如今的智能体技能要么是手工设计、一次性生成，要么通过宽松控制的自我修订演化而来——这些都不像针对技能的深度学习优化器，也都无法在反馈下可靠地优于初始点。我们认为，技能应当被训练为冻结智能体的外部状态，并采用与使权重空间优化可复现相同的规范。就我们所知，SKILLOPT 是首个面向智能体技能的系统化、可控文本空间优化器：一个独立的优化器模型将带评分的 rollout 转化为单个技能文档上的有界增删改编辑，且只有当编辑严格提升留出的验证分数时才会被接受。文本化学习率预算、被拒绝编辑缓冲区以及按 epoch 的慢/元更新，使技能训练稳定，同时在部署时增加零推理阶段模型调用。在六个基准、七个目标模型和三种执行框架（直接对话、Codex、Claude Code）上，SKILLOPT 在全部 52 个评估单元（模型、基准、框架）中表现最佳或持平，并在每个单元上都优于人类、one-shot LLM、Trace2Skill、TextGrad、GEPA 和 EvoSkill 技能中的各自竞争者。在 GPT-5.5 上，它将无技能平均准确率在直接对话中提升 +23.5 个百分点，在 Codex 智能体循环中提升 +24.8 个百分点，在 Claude Code 中提升 +19.1 个百分点。迁移实验进一步表明，优化后的技能产物在跨模型规模、在 Codex 与 Claude Code 执行环境之间迁移，以及迁移到附近的数学基准而无需进一步优化时，仍然保持价值。

                                                                                                              Code: https://aka.ms/SkillOpt 代码：https://aka.ms/SkillOpt

                                                                                                              Correspondence: yifanyang@microsoft.com, yangxue2019-sjtu@sjtu.edu.cn 通信：yifanyang@microsoft.com, yangxue2019-sjtu@sjtu.edu.cn

                                                                                                              • Equal contribution. ‡ Corresponding authors.

                                                                                                              • 共同贡献。 ‡ 通讯作者。

                                                                                                              1 Introduction

                                                                                                              1 引言

                                                                                                              Frontier language models are increasingly deployed as agents, from single-prompt callers to multistep execution harnesses with tools, files, and verifiers [1-4]. In such settings, domain adaptation is no longer only about model weights or prompts: it also requires improving the procedures by which the agent gathers evidence, calls tools, follows domain conventions, and formats outputs [5, 6]. Agent skills provide a natural interface for this procedural adaptation [7, 8]: a skill is a portable natural-language artifact that packages procedures, domain heuristics, tool policies, output constraints, and failure modes, letting a frozen agent adapt through external text. 前沿语言模型正越来越多地被部署为智能体：从单次提示调用者到带工具、文件和验证器的多步执行框架 [1-4]。在这种场景下，领域适配不再只是关于模型权重或提示：还需要改进智能体获取证据、调用工具、遵循领域约定以及格式化输出的流程 [5, 6]。智能体技能为这种程序性适配提供了自然接口 [7, 8]：技能是可移植的自然语言产物，用来打包流程、领域启发式、工具策略、输出约束和失败模式，使冻结的智能体能够通过外部文本进行适应。

                                                                                                              If the recurring object of adaptation is the agent's procedure, the skill document itself should be trainable. Yet weight adaptation is often unavailable for closed frontier models and expensive for open ones, while manually written or one-shot skills are brittle under a target domain or harness. Recent systems convert execution experience into reusable textual artifacts- distilling trajectory lessons, refining skill folders via failure analysis, building domain-specific skill libraries, or optimizing prompts from trajectory feedback [9-13] - but leave open a more basic question: if skills are the adaptation layer, how should they be optimized? Our key idea is to treat skill editing as a controllable domain-adaptation process, with the skill document as the external state, an additional frontier model as the optimizer, and training-style controls over evidence, step size, validation, and update direction. 如果需要反复适配的是智能体的“过程”，那么技能文档本身就应该可以训练。然而，对于封闭的前沿模型，权重适配往往不可用；对开放模型则代价高昂；而手工编写或一次性（one-shot）的技能在目标领域或执行框架下往往又脆弱。近期系统将执行经验转化为可复用的文本产物——提炼轨迹经验教训，通过失败分析精炼技能文件夹，构建领域特定的技能库，或利用轨迹反馈来优化提示 [9-13]——但仍然留着一个更基础的问题：既然技能是适配层，那么应当如何对它们进行优化？我们的核心想法是将“技能编辑”视为一种可控的领域适配过程：以技能文档作为外部状态，再配一个额外的前沿模型作为优化器，并在训练风格的控制下，对证据、步长、验证以及更新方向进行调节。

                                                                                                               

                                                                                                              Figure 1 Overview of SKILLOPT. The target model executes tasks with a current skill, an additional frontier optimizer model converts trajectories into bounded add/delete/replace skill edits, and a held-out gate accepts only edits that improve validation performance. Accepted edits are exported as a reusable skill artifact, while rejected edits become negative feedback for later updates. 图1 展示了 SKILLOPT 的概览。目标模型在当前技能下执行任务；额外的前沿优化器模型将轨迹转换为受限的 add/delete/replace（增删改）技能编辑；然后，一个隔离的门控机制只接受那些能提升验证性能的编辑。被接受的编辑会导出为可复用的技能产物，而被拒绝的编辑则作为后续更新的负反馈。

                                                                                                              We introduce SKILLOPT, a text-space optimizer for agent skills. Given a target domain, an initial skill, and the model being adapted, SKILLOPT repeatedly samples trajectory batches, analyzes successes and failures, and asks a frontier optimizer model to propose structured add/delete/replace edits. It then aggregates and ranks candidate edits under a textual learning-rate budget, applies a bounded update to the skill document, and evaluates the candidate skill on a held-out selection split before accepting it. Rejected edits are retained as negative feedback, while the epoch-wise slow/meta update preserves longer-horizon regularities. Figure 1 gives a schematic view of this loop. The deployed output is a compact best_skill.md file of roughly 300-2,000 tokens, with the adapted model and execution harness remaining fixed. 我们提出 SKILLOPT，这是一种用于智能体技能的文本空间优化器。给定目标领域、初始技能以及正在被适配的模型，SKILLOPT 会反复采样轨迹批次，分析成功与失败，并要求一个前沿优化器模型提出结构化的 add/delete/replace 编辑。随后，它在一个基于文本学习率预算下汇总并对候选编辑进行排序，对技能文档执行受限更新，并在一个隔离的选择划分上评估候选技能，然后再接受它。被拒绝的编辑会保留下来作为负反馈，而逐 epoch 的慢速/元（slow/meta）更新会维持更长时间跨度上的规律。图1 给出了该循环的示意。最终部署输出是一份紧凑的 best_skill.md 文件，约 300-2,000 个 token，并且被适配的模型与执行框架保持不变。

                                                                                                              The deep-learning analogy is operational rather than decorative. Rollout and reflection batch sizes control the noise in the evidence used for each edit; the textual learning rate and schedule control how far one skill version is allowed to move from the previous one; the held-out gate plays the role of validation; and the epoch-wise slow/meta update acts like a momentum term, carrying stable editing directions across epochs. This stability is crucial: if consecutive skill revisions move too far or in inconsistent directions, rejected edits and previous accepted edits no longer provide a meaningful optimization history. With bounded, validation-gated updates, each revision remains close enough to the last one that later optimizer calls can learn from what helped, what failed, and what should be preserved. 该深度学习类比是“可操作”的，而不是为了装饰。回放（rollout）与反思（reflection）的批次规模决定了用于每次编辑的证据中的噪声大小；文本学习率及其调度决定单个技能版本允许从前一个版本移动多远；隔离的门控机制对应验证；而逐 epoch 的慢速/元更新就像动量项一样，在跨 epoch 中携带稳定的编辑方向。这种稳定性至关重要：如果连续的技能修订移动过远，或在不一致的方向上进行，那么被拒绝的编辑与先前被接受的编辑就不再能提供有意义的优化历史。在受限、且经过验证门控的更新下，每次修订都会与上一次保持足够接近，从而让后续优化器调用能从中学习：哪些有效、哪些失败、以及哪些应当被保留。

                                                                                                              We conduct, to our knowledge, the first systematic study of skill optimization as a domain-adaptation training method for frontier agents. We evaluate SKILLOPT on six benchmarks covering QA, spreadsheets, documents, math, and embodied decision making, across seven target models from frontier-scale GPT to small-scale Qwen, and under three execution modes (direct chat, Codex harness, Claude Code harness). Out of 52 evaluated (model, benchmark, harness) cells, SKILLOPT is the best or tied-best measured method on all 52. With GPT-5.5 in direct chat, it lifts SearchQA from 77.7 to 87.3, SpreadsheetBench from 41.8 to 80.7, OfficeQA from 33.1 to 72.1, DocVQA from 78.8 to 91.2, LiveMathematicianBench from 37.6 to 66.9, and ALFWorld from 83.6 to 95.5 (a +23.5 point average gain over no skill), and it also beats the strongest per-cell baseline drawn from human-written, one-shot LLM, Trace2Skill, TextGrad, GEPA, and EvoSkill skills by +5.4 points on average. The same optimization interface is effective inside Codex-style and Claude Code-style execution loops, lifting GPT-5.5 by +24.8 and +19.1 points over no skill respectively, and outperforming EvoSkill by +14.0 and +3.2 points. 据我们所知，我们是首次系统性地研究将技能优化作为一种用于前沿智能体的领域适配训练方法。我们在六个基准上评估 SKILLOPT，覆盖 QA、表格（spreadsheets）、文档（documents）、数学与具身决策（embodied decision making），并在七个目标模型上进行实验，从前沿规模的 GPT 到小规模的 Qwen，同时在三种执行模式下测试（直接对话、Codex 框架、Claude Code 框架）。在共 52 个评估单元（模型-基准-框架）中，SKILLOPT 在所有 52 个单元上都是最优或并列最优。以 GPT-5.5 的直接对话为例，它把 SearchQA 从 77.7 提升到 87.3，把 SpreadsheetBench 从 41.8 提升到 80.7，把 OfficeQA 从 33.1 提升到 72.1，把 DocVQA 从 78.8 提升到 91.2，把 LiveMathematicianBench 从 37.6 提升到 66.9，把 ALFWorld 从 83.6 提升到 95.5（相对无技能基线的 +23.5 分平均增益），并且它也比从人类编写的一次性 LLM 技能（human-written, one-shot LLM）以及 Trace2Skill、TextGrad、GEPA、EvoSkill 中抽取的最强逐单元基线平均高出 +5.4 分。相同的优化接口同样在 Codex 风格与 Claude Code 风格的执行循环内有效：分别让 GPT-5.5 相对无技能提升 +24.8 和 +19.1 分，并且比 EvoSkill 分别高出 +14.0 和 +3.2 分。

                                                                                                              The learned artifacts also transfer beyond the exact training setting. A SpreadsheetBench skill trained on GPT-5.4 improves every smaller GPT variant we test; a Codex-trained spreadsheet skill transfers to Claude Code with a +59.7 point gain; and an OlympiadBench skill yields positive gains on Omni-MATH [14]. These transfer results are important for the paper's application value: a skill can be optimized once, audited as text, and reused across related models, harnesses, or tasks without changing model weights. Our ablations explain why this works. Bounded textual learning outperforms uncontrolled rewriting, held-out gating prevents harmful proposals from accumulating, the rejected-step buffer converts failed edits into negative feedback, and the epoch-wise slow/meta update improves long-horizon refinement without bloating the deployed skill. Finally, per-benchmark case studies show that the learned skills remain compact (300-2,000 tokens after only 1-4 accepted edits), inspectable, and procedural rather than instance-specific. 所学得到的产物也会超越严格的训练设置进行迁移。在 GPT-5.4 上训练的 SpreadsheetBench 技能可提升我们测试的每个更小的 GPT 变体；在 Codex 上训练的表格技能迁移到 Claude Code 时带来 +59.7 分的提升；而 OlympiadBench 技能在 Omni-MATH [14] 上也带来正向收益。这些迁移结果对论文的应用价值很重要：一项技能可以只优化一次，以文本形式审计，并在不改变模型权重的情况下复用于相关模型、执行框架或任务。我们的消融实验解释了其有效原因。受限的文本学习优于不受控制的重写，保留集门控可防止有害提议累积，拒绝步骤缓冲区将失败编辑转化为负反馈，而按 epoch 的慢速/元更新则在不使部署后的技能膨胀的前提下改善长周期细化。最后，按基准的案例研究表明，所学技能仍然紧凑（仅 1-4 次接受编辑后为 300-2,000 个 token），可检查，并且是程序化的而非实例特定的。

                                                                                                              Our contributions are as follows: 我们的贡献如下：

                                                                                                              • We formulate agent-skill learning as optimization over an external natural-language state and introduce SKILLOPT, a harness-agnostic optimizer with rollout batches, reflection minibatches, add/delete/replace edits, textual learning rates, schedules, held-out acceptance, rejected-edit buffers, and epoch-wise slow/meta update.

                                                                                                              • 我们将智能体技能学习表述为在外部自然语言状态上的优化，并提出 SKILLOPT：一种与执行框架无关的优化器，包含 rollout 批次、反思小批次、添加/删除/替换编辑、文本学习率、调度、保留集接受、拒绝编辑缓冲区以及按 epoch 的慢速/元更新。

                                                                                                              • We provide a broad empirical study across six benchmarks, seven target models, and three execution harnesses, showing that SKILLOPT is best or tied-best on 52 of 52 cells and outperforms no-skill, human-skill, one-shot LLM-skill, prompt-optimization (TextGrad, GEPA), and skill-evolution (Trace2Skill, EvoSkill) baselines under every model.

                                                                                                              • 我们在六个基准、七个目标模型和三种执行框架上进行了广泛的实证研究，表明 SKILLOPT 在 52 个单元中的 52 个上达到最佳或并列最佳，并且在所有模型下都优于无技能、人类技能、一次性 LLM 技能、提示优化（TextGrad、GEPA）以及技能演化（Trace2Skill、EvoSkill）基线。

                                                                                                              • We validate the optimization design through component ablations and three forms of transfer (cross-model, cross-harness, cross-benchmark), showing that the exported skill artifact is compact, reusable, and deployable without model-weight updates.

                                                                                                              • 我们通过组件消融和三种迁移形式（跨模型、跨框架、跨基准）验证了该优化设计，表明导出的技能产物紧凑、可复用，并且无需模型权重更新即可部署。

                                                                                                              2 Related Work

                                                                                                              2 相关工作

                                                                                                              Prompt auto tuning and agent-configuration search. GEPA demonstrates that trajectory feedback can guide reflective prompt evolution and outperform reinforcement learning on several language-agent tasks [13]. ABSTRAL and EvoTest extend this idea from single prompts to multi-agent design documents and test-time agentic system evolution without gradients or fine-tuning [15, 16]. By treating language artifacts as optimizable objects, these methods can directly exploit execution feedback, but they mainly target prompts, system designs, or full configurations rather than reusable domain adaptation. SKILLOPT instead optimizes a persistent skill document that can be trained, validated, exported, and reused with the adapted model, applying language-level controllability to a stable procedural skill state. 提示自动调优与代理配置搜索。GEPA 表明，轨迹反馈可以引导反思式提示进化，并在若干语言智能体任务上优于强化学习 [13]。ABSTRAL 和 EvoTest 将这一思路从单个提示扩展到多智能体设计文档，以及无梯度、无微调的测试时智能体系统进化 [15, 16]。通过将语言产物视为可优化对象，这些方法可以直接利用执行反馈，但它们主要针对提示、系统设计或完整配置，而非可复用的领域适配。SKILLOPT 则优化一个持久的技能文档，它可被训练、验证、导出，并与适配后的模型一起复用，将语言层面的可控性应用于稳定的程序性技能状态。

                                                                                                              Skill construction and skill evolution. SkillsBench and the SoK on agentic skills frame skills as reusable procedural knowledge, covering tool policies, applicability conditions, execution routines, and supporting resources [7, 8]. Prior systems construct such skills from lifelong experience, trajectory lessons, skill knowledge bases, or heterogeneous domain resources [9, 12, 17- 19], and further refine them through failure analysis, creation-evaluation-revision loops, coevolving generators and verifiers, collective updates, or reinforcement learning [10, 11, 20-26]. While these works emphasize skill discovery, repository growth, sharing, evolutionary search, or policy optimization, SKILLOPT studies a narrower problem: how to train one compact domain skill with deep-learning-style controls such as trajectory batches, reflection minibatches, textual learning rates, validation gates, rejected-edit buffers, and slow/meta updates. This yields a controlled and auditable procedure for producing a portable best_skill.md without changing model weights. 技能构建与技能进化。SkillsBench 和关于智能体技能的 SoK 将技能视为可复用的程序性知识，涵盖工具策略、适用条件、执行例程和支撑资源 [7, 8]。先前系统从终身经验、轨迹教训、技能知识库或异构领域资源中构建此类技能 [9, 12, 17- 19]，并通过失败分析、创建-评估-修订循环、协同进化的生成器与验证器、集体更新或强化学习进一步细化 [10, 11, 20-26]。尽管这些工作强调技能发现、仓库扩展、共享、进化搜索或策略优化，SKILLOPT 研究的是一个更窄的问题：如何用类似深度学习的控制方式训练一个紧凑的领域技能，例如轨迹批次、反思小批次、文本学习率、验证门、被拒编辑缓冲区和慢/元更新。这提供了一种受控且可审计的流程，用于生成可移植的 best_skill.md，而无需改变模型权重。

                                                                                                               

                                                                                                              Figure 2 Pipeline of SKILLOPT. A frozen target model executes a rollout batch with the current skill; an optimizer model performs minibatch reflection over successes and failures, proposes bounded add/delete/replace edits, merges and ranks them under a scheduled edit budget, and accepts the candidate skill only through a held-out validation gate. Across epochs, the slow/meta update retains longer-horizon lessons without changing the target model. 图 2 SKILLOPT 流程图。冻结的目标模型使用当前技能执行一批 rollout；优化器模型对成功与失败进行小批量反思，提出有界的新增/删除/替换编辑，在预定的编辑预算下进行合并与排序，并仅通过留出验证门接受候选技能。跨越各个 epoch，慢/元更新会保留更长时域的经验，而不改变目标模型。

                                                                                                              3 Method

                                                                                                              3 方法

                                                                                                              3.1 Problem Setup

                                                                                                              3.1 问题建模

                                                                                                              A skill s is a natural-language policy inserted into the agent context before execution,consistent with recent work treating skills as reusable procedural knowledge for agents [7, 8]. In direct-chat benchmarks, it is prepended to the system or developer instruction; in tool-use harnesses, it becomes persistent procedural memory. We use M to denote the frozen target model whose behavior is being adapted through skill optimization. For a harness h ,task x ,and skill s ,execution produces a trajectory τ and a scalar score r : 技能 s 是一种自然语言策略，会在执行前被插入到智能体的上下文中，符合将技能视为可复用的过程性知识的相关工作 [7, 8]。在直接对话基准中，它会被置于系统或开发者指令之前；在工具使用框架中，它会变成持久的过程性记忆。我们用 M 表示被冻结的目标模型，其行为正通过技能优化进行适配。对于一个框架 h 、任务 x 和技能 s ，执行会产生轨迹 τ 与标量得分 r ：

                                                                                                              (1)(τ(s),r(s))=h(M,x,s),r(s)∈[0,1].

                                                                                                               

                                                                                                              Given train,selection,and test splits Dtr,Dsel,D test  ,SKILLOPT uses Dtr to generate a set of candidate skills C(Dtr) ,selects the best skill on D sel  ,and reports the final performance on D test  : 给定训练、选择与测试划分 Dtr,Dsel,D test  ，SKILLOPT 使用 Dtr 生成一组候选技能 C(Dtr) ，在 D sel  上选择最佳技能，并在 D test  上报告最终性能：

                                                                                                              (2)s sel ⋆=arg⁡maxs∈C(Dtr)⁡1|D sel |∑x∈D sel ⁡r(s),

                                                                                                               

                                                                                                              (3)Test(s sel ⋆)=1|D test |∑x∈D test ⁡r(s sel ⋆).

                                                                                                               

                                                                                                              The training split supplies experience, the selection split gates updates, and the test split is used only for final reporting. The optimizer state contains the current skill, the best validation-gated skill, cached skill hashes, an epoch-local rejected-step buffer, and optional slow/meta-update state. Only the best accepted skill is exported as best_skill.md. 训练划分提供经验，选择划分用于控制更新，测试划分仅用于最终汇报。优化器状态包含当前技能、通过验证门控的最佳技能、已缓存的技能哈希、按轮次本地的被拒步缓冲区，以及可选的慢更新/元更新状态。只有通过接受条件的最佳技能会被导出为 best_skill.md。

                                                                                                              3.2 Forward Pass: Rollout Evidence

                                                                                                              3.2 前向传递：滚动证据

                                                                                                              At each optimization step,the target model runs a rollout batch from Dtr with the current skill. The harness records task metadata, messages, tool calls, observations, command outputs, final answers, verifier feedback, and benchmark-specific context such as spreadsheet previews, document references, or compact execution traces. This batch is the evidence unit: small batches update quickly but noisily, while larger batches expose more recurring patterns before the skill changes. The implementation also supports accumulation, where several rollout batches are reflected on separately and merged into one update, decoupling execution throughput from update frequency. 在每个优化步骤中，目标模型都会使用当前技能从 Dtr 运行一个滚动批次。执行器会记录任务元数据、消息、工具调用、观测、命令输出、最终答案、验证器反馈，以及特定基准的上下文，例如电子表格预览、文档引用或紧凑的执行轨迹。这个批次就是证据单元：小批次更新更快但更嘈杂，而大批次在技能变化之前能暴露更多重复出现的模式。实现还支持累积，即将多个滚动批次分别反映后合并为一次更新，从而将执行吞吐量与更新频率解耦。

                                                                                                              3.3 Backward Pass: Minibatch Reflection

                                                                                                              3.3 反向传递：小批量反思

                                                                                                              The optimizer model turns trajectories into skill edits, following the broader line of trajectory-driven reflection and prompt evolution [13, 27, 28]. It first separates failures from successes and partitions each group into reflection minibatches. This matters because single trajectories often produce anecdotal fixes, while minibatches expose reusable procedural errors: the agent consistently searches the wrong source, writes an answer in the wrong format, or fails to verify a tool result. Failure minibatches propose missing or corrective rules; success minibatches preserve behaviors that already work. Each reflection returns structured add/delete/replace edits, or in rewrite mode a small set of rewrite suggestions. 优化器模型将轨迹转化为技能编辑，延续轨迹驱动的反思与提示演化这一更宏观思路[13, 27, 28]。它首先将失败与成功分开，并将每一组划分为反思小批次。这一点很关键：单条轨迹往往只能带来轶事式的修补，而小批次能够暴露可复用的程序性错误——智能体会持续从错误来源检索、以错误格式给出答案，或未能核验工具结果。失败小批次会提出缺失的或纠正性的规则；成功小批次则保留那些已经有效的行为。每次反思都会返回结构化的添加/删除/替换编辑；在改写模式下，则返回一小组改写建议。

                                                                                                              Local proposals are merged hierarchically by first consolidating failure- and success-driven edits separately, then combining them with priority on failure corrections. This step filters duplicate, contradictory, and example-specific suggestions before the optimizer selects the final bounded update. 局部提案会通过先分别合并由失败与成功驱动的编辑、再在优先处理失败修正的前提下将两者合并的方式进行分层整合。在优化器选择最终的有界更新之前，这一步会过滤重复的、互相矛盾的以及过于依赖示例的建议。

                                                                                                              3.4 Bounded Text Updates

                                                                                                              3.4 受限文本更新

                                                                                                              The learning-rate analogue in SKILLOPT is the edit budget Lt : the maximum number of skill edits applied at step t . After aggregation,the optimizer model ranks the merged edit pool by expected utility and clips it to the top Lt edits. This is the key difference from ad hoc prompt rewriting. Unbounded rewrites can erase useful rules, introduce incompatible instructions, or overfit to a local failure; bounded updates preserve continuity while still allowing the skill to acquire new procedures. SKILLOPT supports constant, linear, cosine, and autonomous schedules. The default cosine schedule starts with larger edits and decays toward smaller consolidation steps. 在 SKILLOPT 中，学习率对应项是编辑预算 Lt：即在步骤 t 时应用的技能编辑的最大数量。聚合之后，优化器模型会按期望效用对合并的编辑池进行排序，并将其裁剪为前 Lt 条编辑。这一点是它相较于临时性的提示重写的关键差异。无限制的重写可能会抹除有用规则、引入不兼容的指令，或对局部失败过拟合；受限更新在保持连贯性的同时，仍允许技能习得新的操作流程。SKILLOPT 支持恒定、线性、余弦以及自主式的调度。默认的余弦调度从更大的编辑开始，并衰减到更小的整合步骤。

                                                                                                              The selected edits produce a candidate skill. In patch mode, edits are localized operations such as append, insert, replace, and delete; in rewrite mode, selected suggestions condition a full skill rewrite. Step-level edits cannot overwrite the protected slow-update field, so fast local changes and slower epoch-wise consolidation remain separated. 所选编辑将生成一个候选技能。在补丁模式下，编辑是诸如追加、插入、替换与删除之类的局部操作；在重写模式下，所选建议将用于条件化一次完整的技能重写。步骤级编辑无法覆盖受保护的慢更新字段，因此快速的局部改动与较慢的按轮次整合仍保持分离。

                                                                                                              3.5 Validation Gate and Rejected-Edit Buffer

                                                                                                              3.5 验证门与拒绝编辑缓冲区

                                                                                                              Every candidate skill is evaluated on D sel  with the same frozen target model and harness. If it improves over the current selection score, it becomes the new current skill; if it also exceeds the best score so far, it becomes best_skill.md. Otherwise it is rejected. This gate turns reflection into propose-and-test optimization rather than unconditional self-editing, which is crucial because plausible textual diagnoses can still hurt the actual target model. 每个候选技能都会在D sel 上使用同一个冻结的目标模型和工具链进行评估。如果它优于当前选择分数，就成为新的当前技能；如果它也超过迄今为止的最佳分数，就成为best_skill.md。否则它会被拒绝。这个门控将反思转化为提议与测试式优化，而不是无条件自我编辑，这一点至关重要，因为看似合理的文本诊断仍可能损害实际的目标模型。

                                                                                                              Rejected updates are still useful. The optimizer records an epoch-local buffer containing observed failure patterns and, for rejected steps, the edits that were tried and the score drop they caused. Later reflection calls in the same epoch receive this buffer, so the optimizer model can avoid repeating failed edits and focus on unresolved failures. This gives the loop negative feedback during training without adding inference-time cost. 被拒绝的更新仍然有用。优化器会记录一个仅在当前 epoch 内有效的缓冲区，其中包含观察到的失败模式，以及对于被拒绝步骤，曾尝试过的编辑及其导致的分数下降。后续在同一 epoch 内的反思调用会接收这个缓冲区，因此优化器模型可以避免重复失败的编辑，并专注于尚未解决的失败。这使得循环在训练期间获得负反馈，而不会增加推理时成本。

                                                                                                              3.6 Epoch-Wise Slow/Meta Update

                                                                                                              3.6 基于轮次的慢更新/元更新

                                                                                                              Fast updates learn from the current batch; the epoch-wise slow/meta update learns from adjacent epochs. At the end of an epoch, SKILLOPT samples the same training items under the previous epoch's skill and the current skill, then groups them into improvements, regressions, persistent failures, and stable successes. The optimizer model writes a concise longitudinal guidance block into a protected slow-update field, and this candidate is still passed through the validation gate. Thus slow update captures durable domain lessons while preserving the same safety check as step-level edits. 快速更新从当前批次中学习；基于轮次的慢更新/元更新则从相邻轮次中学习。在每个轮次结束时，SKILLOPT 会在上一轮次的技能与当前技能下对相同的训练样本进行抽样，然后将其分组为改进、退步、持续失败与稳定成功。优化器模型会将一段简洁的纵向指导块写入受保护的慢更新字段，该候选项仍会经过验证门。因此，慢更新在保留与步骤级编辑相同安全检查的同时，能够捕捉持久的领域经验。

                                                                                                              The meta skill is optimizer-side only. It summarizes which edit patterns helped, which were rejected, and which failures persisted across epochs. This meta guidance is prepended to future optimizer prompts for reflection, merging, and ranking, but it is not shipped with the target model. The advantage is separation of concerns: the deployed skill remains compact and portable, while training benefits from a richer record of the editing process. 元技能仅在优化器侧。它会概括哪些编辑模式带来了帮助、哪些被拒绝、以及哪些失败在各轮次间持续存在。这一元指导会被预置到未来优化器提示中，用于反思、融合与排序，但不会随目标模型一起交付。优势在于职责分离：部署后的技能保持精炼且便于携带，而训练则能从更丰富的编辑过程记录中获益。

                                                                                                              3.7 Harness-Agnostic Deployment

                                                                                                              3.7 与执行框架无关的部署

                                                                                                              SKILLOPT is harness-agnostic through a lightweight adapter interface, matching the broader trend toward agents embedded in tool-use and software-execution environments [1, 2, 4]. An adapter constructs train/evaluation batches, injects the current skill into the agent context, runs the native harness, and returns scored trajectories. The same optimizer therefore works for direct QA, spreadsheet execution, document reasoning, multimodal QA, embodied environments, and Codex-style or Claude Code-style execution loops. This is the main practical advantage of treating skills as the adaptation layer: a stronger optimizer model can train a reusable skill artifact offline, and the resulting best_skill.md can then be deployed or tested across target models, harnesses, and nearby benchmarks without changing model weights. SKILLOPT 通过一个轻量级适配器接口实现与执行框架无关，这也契合了代理嵌入工具使用和软件执行环境的更大趋势 [1, 2, 4]。适配器负责构建训练/评估批次，将当前技能注入代理上下文，运行原生执行框架，并返回带评分的轨迹。因此，同一个优化器可用于直接问答、表格执行、文档推理、多模态问答、具身环境，以及 Codex 风格或 Claude Code 风格的执行循环。将技能视为适配层的主要实际优势在于：更强的优化器模型可以离线训练出可复用的技能工件，而生成的 best_skill.md 随后可在目标模型、执行框架和邻近基准上部署或测试，而无需更改模型权重。

                                                                                                              4 Experiments

                                                                                                              4 实验

                                                                                                              We evaluate SKILLOPT as a text-space optimizer for frozen agents: the target model executes each task with the current skill, while an offline optimizer edits that skill from rollout evidence. The experiments answer four questions. (i) Do optimized skills improve over no-skill, human-skill, one-shot LLM-skill, prompt-optimization (TextGrad, GEPA), and skill-evolution (Trace2Skill, EvoSkill) baselines? (ii) Does the same loop work across direct chat, Codex, and Claude Code harnesses, and across seven target models from frontier-scale GPT to small Qwen? (iii) Which optimizer controls matter? (iv) What do the learned skills look like, and at what cost? 我们将 SKILLOPT 评估为针对冻结智能体的文本空间优化器：目标模型在当前技能下执行每个任务，而离线优化器会基于回放证据来编辑该技能。实验回答四个问题。（i）优化后的技能是否优于无技能、人类技能、一次性 LLM 技能、提示优化（TextGrad、GEPA）以及技能演化（Trace2Skill、EvoSkill）等基线？（ii）同一套循环能否在直接对话、Codex 以及 Claude Code 的工具框架中工作，并能否覆盖从前沿规模的 GPT 到小型 Qwen 的七个目标模型？（iii）哪些优化器控制项至关重要？（iv）学到的技能是什么样的，代价又是什么？

                                                                                                              Setting. We report each benchmark's native hard score or exact-match accuracy on held-out test splits across SearchQA [29], SpreadsheetBench [30], OfficeQA [31], DocVQA [32], LiveMathemati-cianBench [33] (abbreviated LiveMath in tables), and ALFWorld [34], using two model families: GPT [35] and Qwen [36, 37]. The benchmark suite is intentionally diverse-it spans single-round QA (SearchQA, DocVQA, LiveMathematicianBench MCQ), multi-turn tool loops with up to 24 tool calls (OfficeQA), multi-round codegen with up to 30 turns and a real openpyxl/pandas runtime (SpreadsheetBench, default mode=multi), and persistent embodied interaction with up to 50 steps per episode (ALFWorld). Dataset-backed runs use deterministic train/selection/test splits derived from the same dataset seed (split_seed = 42); the selection split is used only to accept or reject candidate skill edits, and all reported scores are computed on the disjoint held-out test split. The reported numbers thus measure generalization, not validation-set fit. 设置。我们在 SearchQA [29]、SpreadsheetBench [30]、OfficeQA [31]、DocVQA [32]、LiveMathemati-cianBench [33]（表中缩写为 LiveMath）和 ALFWorld [34] 的保留测试划分上，报告各基准的原生硬分数或精确匹配准确率；使用两类模型家族：GPT [35] 与 Qwen [36, 37]。基准套件刻意保持多样性——它涵盖单轮问答（SearchQA、DocVQA、LiveMathematicianBench MCQ）、最多 24 次工具调用的多轮工具循环（OfficeQA）、最多 30 轮的多轮代码生成，并在真实 openpyxl/pandas 运行环境下执行（SpreadsheetBench，默认模式=multi），以及每个 episode 最多 50 步的持续具身交互（ALFWorld）。基于数据集的运行使用由同一数据集种子生成的确定性训练/选择/测试划分（split_seed = 42）；选择划分仅用于决定接受或拒绝候选技能编辑，而所有报告分数都在互斥的保留测试划分上计算。因此，所报告的数值衡量的是泛化能力，而非验证集拟合。

                                                                                                              Table 1 Main results on held-out test splits. Scores are percentages; within each model-harness block, bold marks the best measured entry and underlining marks the second-best entry for each benchmark. Blue cells denote SKILLOPT, and small green/red subscripts show the absolute change relative to the No skill row of the same model in the same harness. We omit ALFWorld under Codex and Claude Code harnesses because ALFWorld requires persistent embodied-environment interaction. SKILLOPT is the best-or-tied entry on every measured cell of the table, with positive gains over the no-skill baseline throughout. 表 1 在保留测试划分上的主要结果。分数以百分比表示；在每个模型-评测框架块内，加粗标出每个基准的最佳测得条目，下划线标出第二佳条目。蓝色单元表示 SKILLOPT，小的绿色/红色下标表示相对于同一框架中同一模型的“无技能”行的绝对变化。我们在 Codex 和 Claude Code 的评测框架下省略 ALFWorld，因为 ALFWorld 需要持久的具身环境交互。SKILLOPT 在表格每个被测单元格中都是最佳或并列最佳，并且在整个过程中相较于无技能基线均取得正向增益。

                                                                                                              Table 2 Hyperparameter analysis for the text optimizer. Each panel changes one scalar or scheduling factor from the default setting unless noted. Panel (a) fixes the split to 4:1:5 train/selection/test; the 1-example, 20%, 40%, and 80% rows use subsets of the training partition, and the 100% row reuses the completed 4:1:5 split-ratio run. Panel (b) sweeps the reflection mini-batchsize Bm ; panel (c) sweeps the rollout batchsize B . 表2 文本优化器的超参数分析。除非另有说明，每个面板仅更改一个标量或调度因素，其他均保持默认设置不变。面板(a)将划分固定为4:1:5训练/选择/测试；1个示例、20%、40%和80%各行使用训练部分的子集，100%行复用了已完成的4:1:5划分比例运行。面板(b)扫描反思小批量大小Bm；面板(c)扫描rollout批量大小B。

                                                                                                              ComponentSettingSearchQASpreadsheetBenchLiveMath
                                                                                                              Learning-rate formlr=4 (default)87.177.561.3
                                                                                                              dynamic lr85.871.854.0
                                                                                                              without lr84.675.757.3
                                                                                                              Rejected bufferwith rejected buffer87.177.561.3
                                                                                                              without rejected buffer85.572.958.9
                                                                                                              Slow/meta updatemeta skill and slow update87.177.561.3
                                                                                                              without meta skill85.175.758.1
                                                                                                              without meta skill and slow update86.355.059.7
                                                                                                              组件设置SearchQASpreadsheetBenchLiveMath
                                                                                                              学习率形式lr=4（默认）87.177.561.3
                                                                                                              动态学习率85.871.854.0
                                                                                                              无学习率84.675.757.3
                                                                                                              拒绝缓冲区使用拒绝缓冲区87.177.561.3
                                                                                                              不使用拒绝缓冲区85.572.958.9
                                                                                                              慢速/元更新元技能和慢速更新87.177.561.3
                                                                                                              不使用元技能85.175.758.1
                                                                                                              不使用元技能和慢速更新86.355.059.7

                                                                                                              Table 3 Component ablations for learning-rate form, rejected buffer, and epoch-wise slow/meta update. Light-blue rows mark the default setting within each component group; the learning-rate group uses the default lr=4 setting. Bold values mark the best measured result within that group and benchmark. The without-rejected-buffer row uses the matched no-buffer ablation setting. 表3 学习率形式、被拒绝缓冲区与按 epoch 缓慢/meta 更新的组件消融。浅蓝色行标示各组件组中的默认设置；学习率组使用默认 lr=4。加粗数值表示该组与该基准下测得的最佳结果。无被拒绝缓冲区行使用匹配的无缓冲消融设置。

                                                                                                              Default optimizer hyperparameters. Unless noted, SKILLOPT uses four epochs, rollout batch size 40 per step, reflection minibatch size 8 (with 16 analyst workers running reflections in parallel and a merge batch size of 8 ),textual learning rate Lt=4 with cosine decay (floor Lt=2 ,configurable schedules: constant, linear, cosine, autonomous), held-out validation gating (strictly greater than the current selection score-ties are rejected), slow update with 20 sampled tasks per epoch comparing previous-epoch and current-epoch skill, an optimizer-side meta skill that summarizes accepted/rejected patterns into teacher-only guidance, the patch edit mode (the alternative is rewrite_from_suggestions), and an optional rejected-edit buffer of recent failed proposals. Teacher reflection is allowed up to three refinement rounds per minibatch. Both teacher and student calls default to a medium reasoning effort. For benchmarks with tightly bounded training pools (LiveMathematicianBench: 35 training items per epoch with rollout batch 200; ALFWorld: 39 training tasks with 140 selection and 134 test environments), per-benchmark configs scale the batch sizes accordingly while keeping the same gate, scheduler, and slow/meta-update machinery. Additional benchmark, baseline, and optimizer-protocol details are in Appendix C. 默认优化器超参数。除非另有说明，SKILLOPT 使用四个 epoch、每步 rollout 批大小为 40、反思小批大小为 8（16 个 analyst worker 并行运行反思，合并批大小为 8）、文本学习率 Lt=4 及余弦衰减（下限 Lt=2，可配置调度：constant、linear、cosine、autonomous）、保留验证门控（严格大于当前选择分数——平局会被拒绝）、每个 epoch 采样 20 个任务进行缓慢更新，比较上一 epoch 与当前 epoch 的技能、一个优化器侧的 meta 技能，将已接受/已拒绝模式总结为仅供 teacher 使用的指导、patch 编辑模式（另一种是 rewrite_from_suggestions），以及一个可选的近期失败提议被拒绝编辑缓冲区。teacher 反思每个小批最多允许三轮细化。teacher 和 student 调用默认都采用中等推理力度。对于训练池严格受限的基准（LiveMathematicianBench：每个 epoch 35 个训练项，rollout 批大小 200；ALFWorld：39 个训练任务，对应 140 个 selection 和 134 个测试环境），各基准配置会按比例调整批大小，同时保持相同的门控、调度器以及缓慢/meta 更新机制。更多基准、基线和优化器协议细节见附录 C。
