                                                                                                              GPT-5.4-mini34.8-36.6 + 1.8
                                                                                                              GPT-5.4-nano38.8-${40.1} \pm {1.3}$
                                                                                                              （a）跨模型迁移
                                                                                                              源模型目标模型基准基线直接已迁移
                                                                                                              GPT-5.4GPT-5.441.462.5-
                                                                                                              GPT-5.4-miniSpreadsheetBench36.147.545.5+9.4
                                                                                                              GPT-5.4-nano23.542.526.5+3.0
                                                                                                              GPT-5.4GPT-5.436.844.0-
                                                                                                              GPT-5.4-miniLiveMath14.732.819.2 +4.5
                                                                                                              GPT-5.4-nano23.227.228.8 +5.6
                                                                                                              （b）跨测试工具迁移
                                                                                                              源测试工具目标测试工具基准基线直接已迁移
                                                                                                              CodexClaude CodeLiveMath40.856.542.4 +1.6
                                                                                                              Claude CodeCodex35.278.448.0 +12.8
                                                                                                              CodexClaude CodeSpreadsheetBench22.180.481.8 +59.7
                                                                                                              Claude CodeCodex27.585.071.1 +43.6
                                                                                                              （c）跨基准迁移
                                                                                                              源基准目标基准模型基线直接已迁移
                                                                                                              OlympiadBenchOmni-MATHGPT-5.456.6-60.3 + 3.7
                                                                                                              GPT-5.4-mini34.8-36.6 + 1.8
                                                                                                              GPT-5.4-nano38.8-${40.1} \pm {1.3}$

                                                                                                              Table 4 Transfer of optimized skills across three axes. (a) Cross-model: a skill optimized for the source model is deployed on the target model. (b) Cross-harness: a skill trained inside the source harness is evaluated inside the target harness, all on GPT-5.5. (c) Cross-benchmark: the source benchmark skill is evaluated on the target benchmark across three target models. Baseline is the target's no-skill score, Direct is the in-domain SKILLOPT score, and Transferred applies the source skill without further optimization. Subscripts show the change over the target baseline. The GPT-5.4-GPT-5.4 transferred cells in (a) are marked - because source and target match (i.e. no transfer occurs); we still report the GPT-5.4 baseline and in-domain SKILLOPT score (taken from Table 1) in those rows for reference. Every remaining row in (a)-(c) is a positive transfer (no row falls below the target's no-skill baseline). 表4 跨三轴的已优化技能迁移。（a）跨模型：将为源模型优化的技能部署到目标模型。（b）跨工具箱：在源工具箱内训练的技能在目标工具箱内进行评估，且均在 GPT-5.5 上。（c）跨基准：在三种目标模型上，将源基准技能评估到目标基准。基线为目标模型的无技能得分，Direct 为域内 SKILLOPT 得分，Transferred 表示直接应用源技能而不再进行优化。下标显示相对于目标基线的变化。（a）中 GPT-5.4→GPT-5.4 的迁移单元标记为 - ，因为源与目标一致（即发生不发生迁移）；为便于参考，我们仍在这些行中报告 GPT-5.4 基线及域内 SKILLOPT 得分（取自表1）。在（a）至（c）其余每一行中，迁移均为正向（没有任何一行低于目标的无技能基线）。

                                                                                                              Harnesses. Direct chat invokes the target model through a single chat completion call with the skill prepended to the system prompt. The Codex harness drives the target through the codex CLI in a workspace-write sandbox [38]; SKILLOPT renders the current skill to a per-task SKILL.md alongside task files and reads back a compact execution trace (codex_trace_summary.txt) that is included in the teacher reflection context, so the optimizer learns from what the agent actually did, not just its final answer. The Claude Code harness mirrors the same workspace contract through the claude CLI [39]. All three modes consume the same best_skill.md file format, which is what enables the cross-harness transfer experiments in Section 4.3. 工具箱。Direct 通过一次聊天完成调用来触发目标模型：将技能前置到 system 提示中。Codex 工具箱通过 codex CLI 在可写工作区沙盒中驱动目标[38]；SKILLOPT 将当前技能渲染为每个任务的 SKILL.md，放在任务文件旁边，并读取一段紧凑的执行轨迹（codex_trace_summary.txt），将其包含进教师反思上下文中；因此优化器学习的是智能体实际做了什么，而不仅仅是其最终答案。Claude Code 工具箱同样通过 claude CLI [39] 复刻相同的工作区契约。三种模式都消耗同一种最佳技能 best_skill.md 文件格式，这也是第4.3节开展跨工具箱迁移实验的前提。

                                                                                                              Baselines. We compare against seven baselines that span the no-adaptation, hand-written, one-shot, and learning families: no skill (frozen target model run with the benchmark's default system prompt), human skill (an expert-written skill document curated per benchmark), one-shot LLM skill (a single skill generated from a high-level task description by GPT-5.5 and never updated), Trace2Skill [9] (trajectory-level skill distillation), TextGrad [40] (gradient-style natural-language prompt optimization), GEPA [13] (Pareto reflective prompt evolution), and the harness-side competitor EvoSkill [10] (skill-folder evolution under failure analysis). All baselines use the same target model, the same held-out test split, and the same scorer for every benchmark, so the comparison isolates the choice of adaptation procedure rather than secondary factors such as prompt template or scoring pipeline. 基线。我们与七类基线对比，覆盖不适配、人工编写、单次提示以及学习型方法：无技能（冻结的目标模型运行，使用基准的默认 system 提示）、人类技能（每个基准由专家撰写并策划的技能文档）、单次 LLM 技能（由 GPT-5.5 根据高层任务描述生成的单一技能，且从不更新）、Trace2Skill [9]（基于轨迹层面的技能蒸馏）、TextGrad [40]（梯度式自然语言提示优化）、GEPA [13]（帕累托式反思提示演化），以及工具箱侧的对手 EvoSkill [10]（在失败分析下进行技能文件夹演化）。所有基线都使用相同的目标模型、相同的保留测试划分，以及每个基准相同的评分器，因此比较能够将差异归因于适配流程的选择，而非诸如提示模板或评分流水线这类次要因素。

                                                                                                              4.1 Main Results

                                                                                                              4.1 主要结果

                                                                                                              Table 1 is the main result matrix. Counting every (target model, benchmark, harness) cell as one comparison and the strongest of the no-skill, human-skill, LLM-skill, Trace2Skill, TextGrad, GEPA, and EvoSkill baselines as the per-cell competition, SKILLOPT wins or matches the best measured result on 52 of 52 evaluated cells. This dominance is uniform across model scales: SKILLOPT is best on every benchmark for GPT-5.5, GPT-5.4, GPT-5.4-mini, GPT-5.4-nano, GPT-5.2, Qwen3.5-4B, and Qwen3.6-35B-A3B in direct chat, and for GPT-5.5 under both Codex and Claude Code harnesses. 表1是主要结果矩阵。将每个（目标模型、基准、harness）单元格算作一次比较，并以 no-skill、human-skill、LLM-skill、Trace2Skill、TextGrad、GEPA 和 EvoSkill 基线中每个单元格最强者作为对手，SKILLOPT 在 52/52 个评估单元格中胜出或持平最佳结果。这种优势在不同模型规模上一致：在直接聊天中，SKILLOPT 在 GPT-5.5、GPT-5.4、GPT-5.4-mini、GPT-5.4-nano、GPT-5.2、Qwen3.5-4B 和 Qwen3.6-35B-A3B 的每个基准上都是最佳；在 Codex 和 Claude Code 两种 harness 下，GPT-5.5 也同样如此。

                                                                                                              The size of the gains is also unusually large for a no-weight-update method. On GPT-5.5 direct chat, the six-benchmark average rises from 58.8 (no skill) to 82.3 (SKILLOPT), a +23.5 point absolute improvement, while the best per-cell baseline averages only 76.9, leaving SKILLOPT +5.4 points clear of an oracle baseline that picks the best of six competing methods per cell. Per-benchmark deltas over no skill range from +9.6 on SearchQA, where the no-skill model is already near ceiling, to +38.9 on SpreadsheetBench and +39.0 on OfficeQA, where strict procedural and answer-format requirements expose the limits of zero-shot frontier models. Procedural benchmarks see the largest improvements: SpreadsheetBench 41.8 →80.7 ,OfficeQA 33.1 →72.1 , and LiveMathematicianBench 37.6 →66.9 on GPT-5.5; SpreadsheetBench 9.3 →23.9(×2.6) on Qwen3.5-4B; and ALFWorld 34.3-69.4 (×2.0) on GPT-5.4-nano. 这种提升幅度对一种不更新权重的方法而言也异常大。在 GPT-5.5 直接聊天中，六项基准的平均分从 58.8（no skill）升至 82.3（SKILLOPT），绝对提升 +23.5 分；而每单元格最佳基线的平均值仅为 76.9，这使得 SKILLOPT 比一个在每个单元格中从六种竞争方法里选最佳者的 oracle 基线还高出 +5.4 分。相较 no skill 的各基准增量从 SearchQA 的 +9.6 到 SpreadsheetBench 的 +38.9 和 OfficeQA 的 +39.0 不等；在后两者中，严格的流程与答案格式要求暴露了零样本前沿模型的局限。流程类基准的提升最大：GPT-5.5 上 SpreadsheetBench 为 41.8 →80.7 、OfficeQA 为 33.1 →72.1 、LiveMathematicianBench 为 37.6 →66.9；Qwen3.5-4B 上 SpreadsheetBench 为 9.3 →23.9(×2.6)；GPT-5.4-nano 上 ALFWorld 为 34.3-69.4（×2.0）。

                                                                                                              The improvement is not specific to frontier-scale targets. Averaged over the six benchmarks, SKILLOPT lifts GPT-5.4 by +12.7 points, GPT-5.4-mini by +15.4, GPT-5.4-nano by +26.7, GPT-5.2 by +16.6, Qwen3.5-4B by +19.2, and Qwen3.6-35B-A3B by +9.1, for an average improvement of approximately +17.6 points per model. Small and weak target models benefit the most in relative terms (e.g. GPT-5.4-nano nearly doubles on DocVQA and triples on ALFWorld), which is consistent with the view that a compact skill artifact can supply procedural knowledge that small models do not yet hold in weights. 这种提升并不局限于前沿规模目标模型。按六项基准平均，SKILLOPT 使 GPT-5.4 提升 +12.7 分，GPT-5.4-mini 提升 +15.4，GPT-5.4-nano 提升 +26.7，GPT-5.2 提升 +16.6，Qwen3.5-4B 提升 +19.2，Qwen3.6-35B-A3B 提升 +9.1，平均每个模型约提升 +17.6 分。较小且较弱的目标模型在相对意义上获益最大（例如 GPT-5.4-nano 在 DocVQA 上几乎翻倍，在 ALFWorld 上接近三倍），这与“一个紧凑的技能工件可以为尚未在权重中掌握程序性知识的小模型提供这类知识”的观点一致。

                                                                                                              The same optimization interface is also effective under tool-backed execution. On the Codex harness, SKILLOPT is best on all five evaluated benchmarks for GPT-5.5, with average gain +24.8 points over no skill and +14.0 over the next-best baseline (EvoSkill). On the Claude Code harness, it is best on all five benchmarks for GPT-5.5, with average gain +19.1 over no skill and +3.2 over EvoSkill, while EvoSkill itself already lifts the five-benchmark average from 57.8 to 73.7. The two ALFWorld cells under harness rows are left blank because ALFWorld requires persistent embodied-environment interaction that is not represented in the standard Codex / Claude Code adapters; we therefore report harness results on search, spreadsheets, document QA, multimodal QA, and math. 同一优化接口在有工具支持的执行中也同样有效。在 Codex harness 上，SKILLOPT 在 GPT-5.5 的五个已评估基准上均为最佳，相比 no skill 平均提升 +24.8 分，比次优基线（EvoSkill）高 +14.0 分。在 Claude Code harness 上，它在 GPT-5.5 的五个基准上也全部最佳，相比 no skill 平均提升 +19.1 分，比 EvoSkill 高 +3.2 分，而 EvoSkill 本身已将五项基准平均分从 57.8 提升到 73.7。harness 行中的两个 ALFWorld 单元格留空，是因为 ALFWorld 需要持续的具身环境交互，而标准的 Codex / Claude Code 适配器并不体现这一点；因此我们报告的 harness 结果涵盖搜索、电子表格、文档问答、多模态问答和数学。

                                                                                                              Taken together, the table supports a strong empirical claim: across direct chat and two tool-execution harnesses, across seven target models, and on procedural and factual benchmarks alike, optimizing a single compact skill artifact under bounded text-space training is the strongest no-weight-update adaptation strategy among the baselines we consider. The main gains come from feedback-driven skill editing rather than from a better one-shot prompt: human and LLM skills can help when prior instructions happen to match the benchmark, but they cannot correct failures after observing rollouts; Trace2Skill mines trajectory lessons without a held-out gate; TextGrad and GEPA optimize prompts but not a persistent skill artifact; and EvoSkill, the strongest harness-side competitor, lacks both bounded textual learning rates and rejected-edit memory. These comparisons support the central design choice-keep the target model, harness, and evaluator fixed, and optimize only the reusable skill artifact. 综上，该表有力支持一个经验性结论：在直接对话和两个工具执行评测基准中，在七个目标模型上，以及在程序性与事实性基准上，针对受限文本空间训练优化单一紧凑技能工件，是我们所考虑基线中最强的无权重更新适配策略。主要收益来自基于反馈的技能编辑，而非更好的“一次性提示”：当先前指令恰好与基准匹配时，人类与LLM技能能有所帮助，但它们无法在观察到回合结果后纠正失败；Trace2Skill无需外部的隔离门也能挖掘轨迹经验；TextGrad和GEPA优化的是提示而非持续的技能工件；而EvoSkill（最强的基准端竞争者）既没有受限的文本学习率，也缺少被拒绝编辑的记忆。这些对比支持核心设计选择：固定目标模型、评测工具与评估器，仅优化可复用的技能工件。

                                                                                                              Alternative explanations. The per-cell baselines clarify what drives the gains. The effect is not simply prompt length: human skills are already 145-516 tokens long and often exceed the one-shot LLM skill, yet they are beaten in every direct-chat model row while the learned artifacts remain compact (Table 6). It is also not only optimizer capacity: SKILLOPT leads every baseline even for GPT-5.4-nano, and the optimizer-strength analysis in Table 5 shows that a target-matched optimizer recovers much of the gain. Finally, the harness results show that the method is not just exploiting one skill format: EvoSkill already improves the Codex SpreadsheetBench cell from 27.5 to 67.5,but SKILLOPT adds another +17.5 points (67.5→85.0). The gains are largest on procedural benchmarks, where reusable rules about tool use and output formatting matter most, but they also appear on factual and multimodal benchmarks. 其他可能解释。逐格基线有助于澄清收益来自哪里。效果并非仅由提示长度决定：人类技能本身已长达145-516个token，且往往超过“一次性”LLM技能，但在每一行直接对话模型中都仍被击败，而学到的工件依旧保持紧凑（表6）。这也不只是优化器容量：即便是GPT-5.4-nano，SKILLOPT也能胜过每个基线；表5的优化器强度分析进一步表明，匹配目标的优化器能挽回大量收益。最后，基准结果还表明该方法不仅在利用某一种技能格式：EvoSkill已将Codex SpreadsheetBench单元从27.5提升到67.5，但SKILLOPT再增加+17.5分（67.5→85.0）。收益在程序性基准上最大——因为关于工具使用与输出格式的可复用规则最关键——但在事实性与多模态基准上也同样出现。

                                                                                                              Headline numbers in one place. For convenience, the headline aggregates over Table 1 are: (i) 52/52 cells best or tied-best; (ii) average per-model improvement ≈+17.6 points across the seven direct-chat target models; (iii) average GPT-5.5 improvement of +23.5 (direct chat), +24.8 (Codex), +19.1 (Claude Code) over no skill; (iv) GPT-5.5 oracle-baseline gap of +5.4 points (direct chat) computed as the difference between SKILLOPT's six-benchmark average (82.3) and an oracle that picks the best of six competing methods per cell (76.9). The remainder of this section unpacks why these gains appear (Section 4.2), how stable and transferable they are (Section 4.3), and what the learned artifact looks like (Section 4.4). 把关键数字放在一起。为方便起见，表1的汇总要点如下：(i) 52/52个单元最优或并列最优；(ii) 七个直接对话目标模型上的平均每模型提升≈+17.6分；(iii) 相较无技能，GPT-5.5的平均提升为+23.5（直接对话）、+24.8（Codex）、+19.1（Claude Code）；(iv) GPT-5.5的oracle-基线差距为+5.4分（直接对话），计算方式为：SKILLOPT六个基准的平均值（82.3）与选择每个单元六种竞争方法中最优者的oracle（76.9）之间的差。接下来本节将展开这些收益为何出现（第4.2节）、其稳定性与可迁移性（第4.3节），以及所学到的工件长什么样（第4.4节）。

                                                                                                              4.2 Ablations

                                                                                                              4.2 消融实验

                                                                                                              Table 2, Figure 3, and Table 3 test the design choices in the optimizer using GPT-5.5 as both the target and the optimizer. The overall message is that SKILLOPT benefits from sufficient evidence, a bounded textual learning rate, rejected-edit feedback, and epoch-wise slow/meta update. SearchQA has limited headroom and is therefore stable across many settings (most cells fluctuate inside a ±1.5 point band),while SpreadsheetBench and LiveMathematicianBench expose the trade-off between learning useful procedures and over-editing the skill. 表 2、图 3 和表 3 用 GPT-5.5 同时作为目标与优化器来检验优化器中的设计选择。总体信息是：SKILLOPT 受益于足够的证据、有界的文本学习率、拒绝式编辑反馈，以及按轮次进行的慢更新/元更新。SearchQA 的可改进空间有限，因此在许多设置下都很稳定（多数单元格在 ±1.5 个点的范围内波动），而 SpreadsheetBench 和 LiveMathematicianBench 则揭示了在学习有用流程与过度编辑技能之间的权衡。

                                                                                                              Evidence and batch sizes (panels a, b, c). Panel (a) shows that procedural benchmarks reward more training evidence: SpreadsheetBench climbs 47.5 →78.0 and LiveMathematicianBench climbs 59.1→70.5 as the optimizer sees 1→100% of the training partition,while SearchQA saturates at roughly 84-86 after 20% already. Panel (b) shows the same robustness in the other direction: varying the reflection mini-batchsize from 1 to 32 keeps SearchQA inside 85.9-87.1 and Spread-sheetBench inside 75.4-77.9,with the default Bm=8 at or near the top on all three benchmarks. Panel (c) is equally flat in the rollout-batchsize dimension-moving from B=8 to a full epoch keeps SearchQA inside 85.1-87.2 and SpreadsheetBench inside 75.0-77.5. Together this means the headline gains are not the product of a fragile prompt-search batch size, but a genuine effect of having enough scored evidence per update. 证据与批大小（子图 a、b、c）。子图 (a) 表明：程序类基准奖励更多的训练证据。随着优化器看到训练划分中的 1→100%，SpreadsheetBench 提升 47.5 →78.0，LiveMathematicianBench 提升 59.1→70.5；而 SearchQA 在已到约 20% 时就几乎饱和于 84-86。子图 (b) 体现了同样的鲁棒性：将反思小批量大小从 1 变到 32，能让 SearchQA 保持在 85.9-87.1，SpreadsheetBench 保持在 75.4-77.9；默认 Bm=8 在三个基准上都处于最高水平或接近最高。子图 (c) 在 rollout 批大小维度上同样平坦：从 B=8 变到一个完整 epoch，SearchQA 保持在 85.1-87.2，SpreadsheetBench 保持在 75.0-77.5。综合来看，这意味着标题式增益并非来自脆弱的提示-搜索批大小，而是每次更新都有足够的带分数证据所带来的真实效果。

                                                                                                              Textual learning rate and schedule (panels d, e). Panels (d) and (e) directly compare bounded textual learning to looser settings. Sweeping Lt∈{1,2,4,8,16} shows that small or moderate edit budgets are competitive throughout: Lt=4 achieves 86.5/78.2/56.5 ,the highest LiveMath score belongs to Lt=8 at 66.9,and the lowest score across all five settings is still only 85.5 on SearchQA. Panel (e) confirms this on the schedule axis: constant decay scores 87.3/80.7/62.1, cosine 87.1/77.5/61.3, and linear 87.2/72.9/62.9, so the bounded-update story does not depend on a single specific scheduler. The qualitative claim is what matters: any moderate, bounded edit budget already beats baselines that rewrite the skill without a budget (Table 3, "without lr" row, 84.6/75.7/57.3). 文本学习率与调度（子图 d、e）。子图 (d) 和 (e) 直接对比有界的文本学习与更宽松的设置。扫过 Lt∈{1,2,4,8,16} 后可以看到：较小或中等的编辑预算在整个过程中都具有竞争力。Lt=4 达到 86.5/78.2/56.5，最高的 LiveMath 分数属于 Lt=8，为 66.9；所有五种设置中的最低分在 SearchQA 上也只有 85.5。子图 (e) 在调度轴上进一步验证这一点：恒定衰减得到 87.3/80.7/62.1，cosine 得到 87.1/77.5/61.3，linear 得到 87.2/72.9/62.9，因此“有界更新”的解释并不依赖于某一种特定的调度器。真正重要的是质性结论：任何中等、有界的编辑预算，都会已经胜过那些不受预算限制、直接重写技能的基线（表 3，"without lr" 行，84.6/75.7/57.3）。

                                                                                                              Epoch-wise slow/meta update (panel f, Table 3, Figure 3). The slow/meta update supplies longer-horizon guidance beyond the current rollout batch. Slow-update sampling (panel f) places the default at 20 examples per epoch (87.1, 77.5, and 61.3), with 5, 10, and 40 each within ±2.7 points. In the matched default component row,removing the rejected-edit buffer lowers scores by 1.6, 4.6, and 2.4 points on SearchQA, SpreadsheetBench, and LiveMath, respectively, supporting its role as a stabilizer for the default loop rather than as an extra deployment-time mechanism. The slow/meta ablation rows are sharper: removing both meta skill and slow update drops SpreadsheetBench from 77.5 to 55.0 (-22.5 points), the largest degradation in the ablation suite. Figure 3 complements these numerical ablations: validation checkpoints track held-out test performance across epochs, confirming that the gate tends to select skills that generalize rather than skills that only fit the selection split. 按轮次的慢更新/元更新（子图 f、表 3、图 3）。慢更新/元更新提供了超出当前 rollout 批次的更长视野指导。慢更新采样（子图 f）将默认值设为每个 epoch 20 个示例（87.1、77.5 和 61.3），其中 5、10 和 40 各自也都落在 ±2.7 个点内。在匹配的默认组件行中，移除被拒绝编辑缓冲会分别让 SearchQA、SpreadsheetBench 和 LiveMath 的分数下降 1.6、4.6 和 2.4 分，说明它在默认循环中起的是稳定器作用，而不是额外的部署时机制。慢更新/元更新的消融行更为尖锐：移除元技能与慢更新都会让 SpreadsheetBench 从 77.5 降到 55.0（-22.5 分），这是消融套件中最大的退化。图 3 也补充了这些数值消融：验证检查点在各个 epoch 上跟踪持出测试性能，表明门控倾向于选择能泛化的技能，而不是只适配选择划分的技能。

                                                                                                               

                                                                                                              Figure 3 Performance trends across epoch checkpoints on three benchmarks: (a) SpreadsheetBench, (b) SearchQA, and (c) LiveMath. For each checkpoint, we report the training rollout score, the selection-best score on the validation set, and the final performance on the unseen test set. The results show how skill quality evolves during optimization and whether the checkpoint preferred by validation selection aligns with the checkpoint that yields the best generalization to the test set. 图 3：三个基准在各个 epoch 检查点上的性能趋势：(a) SpreadsheetBench、(b) SearchQA、(c) LiveMath。对每个检查点，我们报告训练 rollout 得分、验证集上的选择最优得分，以及在未见测试集上的最终性能。结果展示了技能质量在优化过程中如何演化，以及由验证选择偏好的检查点，是否与产生最佳测试集泛化的检查点一致。

                                                                                                              Gate strictness and edit observability. The validation gate is intentionally strict: a candidate skill is accepted only when its selection-split score is strictly greater than the current selection score, so ties are rejected and the deployed skill never silently drifts. This conservative criterion makes rejected edits informative negative feedback rather than hidden state. Operationally, every step also records an edit_apply_report.json containing per-edit accept/skip status, so the source of every change to best_skill.md is recoverable after the fact. The epoch-wise slow/meta update writes into a markup-fenced protected region of the skill document that step-level edits cannot overwrite, separating the fast intra-epoch update from the slower cross-epoch consolidation; the optimizer-side meta skill lives only in the teacher's reflection context and is never shipped with the deployed artifact. These implementation choices explain why removing both meta skill and slow update is especially damaging on SpreadsheetBench: it removes the long-horizon evidence stream and the protected-region contract that keeps local edits from overwriting durable procedural lessons. 门控严格性与编辑可观测性。验证门控刻意设得很严格：只有当候选技能的 selection-split 分数严格高于当前 selection 分数时才会被接受，因此平局会被拒绝，已部署的技能也绝不会无声漂移。这一保守准则使被拒绝的编辑成为有信息量的负反馈，而非隐藏状态。操作上，每一步还会记录一个 edit_apply_report.json，其中包含每次编辑的接受/跳过状态，因此事后可追溯 best_skill.md 每次变更的来源。按 epoch 的慢速/meta 更新会写入技能文档中一个带标记围栏保护的区域，步骤级编辑无法覆盖，从而将 epoch 内的快速更新与跨 epoch 的较慢整合分离开来；优化器侧的 meta skill 只存在于教师的反思上下文中，绝不会随部署产物一同发布。这些实现选择解释了为什么同时移除 meta skill 和 slow update 在 SpreadsheetBench 上尤其有害：它去除了长期证据流，以及防止局部编辑覆盖持久过程性经验的受保护区域契约。

                                                                                                              Overall, the ablations show that the gains are relatively insensitive to the exact rollout batch, reflection minibatch, or learning-rate schedule, but much more sensitive to the presence of bounded text-space learning, validation gating, rejected-edit feedback, and epoch-wise slow/meta update-the design choices that make skill editing behave like a controlled training loop. 总体而言，消融结果表明，收益对精确的 rollout batch、reflection minibatch 或学习率调度相对不敏感，但对有界文本空间学习、验证门控、被拒绝编辑反馈以及按 epoch 的 slow/meta update 的存在要敏感得多——这些设计使技能编辑表现得像一个受控的训练循环。

                                                                                                              4.3 Analysis and Transfer

                                                                                                              4.3 分析与迁移

                                                                                                              Tables 4-4 ask whether an optimized skill behaves like a reusable artifact rather than a task-specific prompt. We test three shifts: deploying a skill across model scales (Table 4), moving it across execution harnesses (Table 4), and applying it to nearby math benchmarks, including OlympiadBench [41] and Omni-MATH [14] (Table 4). Table 5 then asks how much of the gain depends on optimizer capacity by replacing the frontier optimizer with a target-matched one of the same scale as the deployed model. 表 4-4 询问：被优化的技能，是否更像可复用的产物，而不是特定任务的提示词。我们测试三种变化：将技能跨模型规模部署（表 4）、将其跨执行工具框架迁移（表 4），以及将其应用到相邻的数学基准，包括奥林匹克竞赛基准 OlympiadBench [41] 和 Omni-MATH [14]（表 4）。随后表 5 再问：收益有多少取决于优化器能力——通过把前沿优化器替换为一个目标匹配的优化器（规模与部署模型相同）。

                                                                                                              Cross-model transfer. Table 4(a) reports four genuine cross-model transfers (the GPT-5.4 → GPT- 5.4 entries are kept only as in-domain references and marked - in the transferred column, since source and target match): SpreadsheetBench skills trained on GPT-5.4 transfer to GPT-5.4-mini (+9.4) and GPT-5.4-nano (+3.0), and LiveMath skills transfer to GPT-5.4-mini (+4.5) and GPT- 5.4-nano (+5.6). All four cross-model rows are positive, and on one of them the transferred skill surpasses the in-domain SKILLOPT reference (LiveMath GPT-5.4-nano: 28.8 transferred vs. 27.2 in-domain), suggesting that some learned procedures are target-model agnostic. The remaining cross-model rows still recover a useful fraction of the in-domain gain -e.g. SpreadsheetBench GPT-5.4-mini retains 82% of the in-domain gain (+9.4 of +11.4) and no row falls below the target's no-skill baseline. 跨模型迁移。表 4(a) 报告了四条真正的跨模型迁移（GPT-5.4 → 的 GPT- 5.4 条目仅作为域内参照保留，并在迁移列中标注——由于源模型与目标模型匹配）：在 GPT-5.4 上训练的 SpreadsheetBench 技能迁移到 GPT-5.4-mini（+9.4）和 GPT-5.4-nano（+3.0）；LiveMath 技能则迁移到 GPT-5.4-mini（+4.5）和 GPT- 5.4-nano（+5.6）。四条跨模型结果均为正，其中一条迁移后的技能超过了域内的 SKILLOPT 参照（LiveMath GPT-5.4-nano：迁移 28.8 对比域内 27.2），这表明部分学到的流程与目标模型无关。其余跨模型行仍能恢复域内收益的一部分——例如 SpreadsheetBench 在 GPT-5.4-mini 上保留了 82% 的域内收益（+9.4 / +11.4），且没有任何一条低于目标的无技能基线。

                                                                                                              Cross-harness transfer. The harness-shift rows in Table 4(b) are the clearest deployment signal. A SpreadsheetBench skill trained inside the Codex loop transfers to Claude Code with absolute gain +59.7 over the Claude Code no-skill baseline (22.1 →81.8 ,slightly exceeding the in-domain Claude Code SKILLOPT reference of 80.4), and the symmetric Claude-Code → Codex transfer adds +43.6 on top of the Codex baseline (27.5→71.1). On LiveMath, the Codex→Claude Code transfer is smaller (+1.6 over a 40.8 baseline) but still positive, while the Claude-Code → Codex transfer adds +12.8 (35.2 →48.0 ). Because the two harnesses expose different tool/file APIs and command surfaces, these positive transfers suggest that the learned rules are not only harness-specific command recipes. In SpreadsheetBench especially, the transferred skill appears to encode workbook-level procedures such as structure-first inspection, formula-aware verification, and static-value materialization, so the cost of optimizing a skill in one execution environment can be amortized across related deployment environments. 跨工具框架迁移。表 4(b) 中“框架变更”相关的行，是最清晰的部署信号。在 Codex 循环内部训练的 SpreadsheetBench 技能，迁移到 Claude Code 后相对 Claude Code 无技能基线带来绝对收益 +59.7（22.1 →81.8 ，略高于域内 Claude Code 的 SKILLOPT 参照 80.4）；对称的 Claude-Code → Codex 迁移在 Codex 基线之上再加 +43.6（27.5→71.1）。在 LiveMath 上，Codex→Claude Code 的迁移更小（相对 40.8 的基线为 +1.6），但仍为正；而 Claude-Code → Codex 迁移再加 +12.8（35.2 →48.0 ）。由于这两个框架暴露了不同的工具/文件 API 与命令界面，这些正向迁移表明，学到的规则不仅仅是框架特定的指令配方。尤其在 SpreadsheetBench 中，迁移后的技能似乎编码了工作簿级流程，如先结构后检查、公式感知的验证，以及静态值的物化；因此，在一个执行环境中优化技能的成本，可以在相关的部署环境中摊销。

                                                                                                              Cross-benchmark transfer. Cross-benchmark transfer is the strictest of the three shifts: source and target benchmarks share only the broad task family (math). On the OlympiadBench → Omni-MATH direction reported in Table 4(c), the transferred skill is positive on all three model scales we evaluate, with gains of +3.7 on GPT-5.4, +1.8 on GPT-5.4-mini, and +1.3 on GPT-5.4-nano. These rows are smaller than the in-domain and cross-harness transfers unsurprisingly, since they require the optimized skill to retain useful procedural knowledge after both the test instances and the answer-format conventions change - but they remain uniformly positive, supporting the intended interpretation that the optimized skill encodes reusable mathematical procedure rather than memorized benchmark-specific formatting. 跨基准迁移。跨基准迁移是三种变化中最严格的一种：源基准与目标基准仅共享广义任务家族（数学）。在表 4(c) 报告的 OlympiadBench → 向 Omni-MATH 方向上，被迁移的技能在我们评估的三种模型规模上都为正，增益分别为：GPT-5.4 +3.7、GPT-5.4-mini +1.8、GPT-5.4-nano +1.3。正如预期，这些行小于域内迁移与跨工具框架迁移，因为它要求在测试实例与答案格式约定都发生变化后，仍保留有用的程序性知识——但结果依然一致为正。它支持我们期望的解释：被优化的技能编码的是可复用的数学程序，而不是记忆了特定基准的格式化内容。

                                                                                                              Effect of optimizer strength. Because the optimizer in SKILLOPT runs only during the offline training loop and is never invoked at deployment, optimizer choice is a training-time lever: a stronger optimizer can improve the deployed skill without raising the inference cost of using that skill. The deployed artifact is still a static best_skill.md that calls only the target model. Table 5 quantifies this lever by running the same loop with two optimizer regimes—a strong frontier optimizer (GPT-5.5) and a target-matched optimizer that shares the target model-while holding the rollout batches, validation gate, bounded edit budget, rejected-edit buffer, and slow/meta update identical. 优化器强度的影响。由于 SKILLOPT 中的优化器只在离线训练循环期间运行，从不在部署时调用，因此优化器选择是一个训练时杠杆：更强的优化器可以提升部署后的技能，而不会增加使用该技能的推理成本。部署后的产物仍然是一个静态的 best_skill.md，只会调用目标模型。表 5 通过在两种优化器策略下运行相同的循环量化了这一杠杆——一种是强前沿优化器（GPT-5.5），另一种是目标匹配的优化器：同时保持 rollout 批次、验证门控、受限编辑预算、被拒绝编辑缓冲区以及慢更新/元更新设置完全相同，仅替换优化器。

                                                                                                              Two observations follow. First, the stronger optimizer produces larger absolute gains on every (benchmark, target) cell we test: GPT-5.4-nano lifts by +19.0 vs. +11.9 on SpreadsheetBench and +19.0 vs. +14.1 on SearchQA, and GPT-5.4-mini follows the same ordering (+11.4 vs. +7.1 on SpreadsheetBench, +4.3 vs. +2.4 on SearchQA). The bounded-edit, validation-gated loop is what makes this monotone: without the gate, a stronger optimizer could just as easily push larger but harmful rewrites. Second, the target-matched optimizer is far from collapsed-it recovers 56-74% of the strong-optimizer gain across the four cells, confirming that SKILLOPT is not a distillation pipeline from a stronger teacher into a weaker student: the optimization loop itself contributes substantial value on top of whatever the optimizer can already do. The practical implication is that a high-capacity frontier optimizer is the right default whenever it is available-it costs only training-time API calls and adds nothing to deployment-while the same loop remains effective if the budget forces a target-matched optimizer instead. 有两个观察。首先，更强的优化器在我们测试的每个（基准，目标）单元上都带来更大的绝对增益：GPT-5.4-nano 在 SpreadsheetBench 上提升 +19.0，对比 +11.9，在 SearchQA 上提升 +19.0，对比 +14.1；GPT-5.4-mini 也遵循同样的排序（SpreadsheetBench 上 +11.4 对比 +7.1，SearchQA 上 +4.3 对比 +2.4）。这种单调性正是由受限编辑、验证门控的循环所带来的：如果没有这个门控，更强的优化器完全可能进行更大但有害的改写。其次，目标匹配的优化器远未失效——它在四个单元中恢复了强优化器增益的 56-74%，这也证实 SKILLOPT 不是把更强教师蒸馏成更弱学生的流水线：优化循环本身就在优化器原本能做的基础上提供了可观价值。实际意义在于，只要高容量的前沿优化器可用，它就是默认的最佳选择——它只会增加训练阶段的 API 调用，而不会增加部署成本；而当预算迫使使用目标匹配的优化器时，这一循环同样依然有效。

                                                                                                              BenchmarkTargetBaselineTarget-matched optimizer
                                                                                                              SpreadsheetBenchGPT-5.4-mini36.147.5+11.443.2 +7.1
                                                                                                              GPT-5.4-nano23.542.5+19.035.4 +11.9
                                                                                                              SearchQAGPT-5.4-mini75.980.2 +4.378.3 +2.4
                                                                                                              GPT-5.4-nano55.874.8 +19.069.9 +14.1
                                                                                                              基准目标基线目标匹配优化器
                                                                                                              SpreadsheetBenchGPT-5.4-mini36.147.5+11.443.2 +7.1
                                                                                                              GPT-5.4-nano23.542.5+19.035.4 +11.9
                                                                                                              SearchQAGPT-5.4-mini75.980.2 +4.378.3 +2.4
                                                                                                              GPT-5.4-nano55.874.8 +19.069.9 +14.1

                                                                                                              Table 5 Effect of optimizer strength. Each (benchmark, target) pair is optimized either by a strong frontier optimizer (GPT-5.5, bolded) or by a target-matched optimizer that shares the target model; everything else in the SKILLOPT loop is held fixed. Gains over the target's no-skill baseline are shown as small green subscripts; the same baseline is used for both optimizer settings within a row. The optimizer runs only during offline training, so the stronger-optimizer column adds zero cost at deployment. 表 5 优化器强度的影响。每个（基准、目标）对要么通过强前沿优化器（GPT-5.5，粗体）进行优化，要么通过与目标模型匹配的优化器进行优化；其余所有内容都在 SKILLOPT 循环中保持不变。相对于目标的无技能基线的提升以小的绿色下标表示；同一行内两种优化器设置都使用相同的基线。优化器仅在离线训练阶段运行，因此更强的优化器列在部署时不增加额外成本。

                                                                                                              BenchmarkInitial (tok)Final (tok)EditsTrain tokensCost / pt
                                                                                                              SearchQA168574213.8M37.9M
                                                                                                              SpreadsheetBench2241,995421.4M0.6M
                                                                                                              OfficeQA145883120.8M1.1M
                                                                                                              DocVQA819593188.2M46.4M
                                                                                                              LiveMath154379123.2M3.6M
                                                                                                              ALFWorld5161,321259.3M15.9M
                                                                                                              基准初始（词元）最终（词元）编辑训练词元成本 / pt
                                                                                                              SearchQA168574213.8M37.9M
                                                                                                              SpreadsheetBench2241,995421.4M0.6M
                                                                                                              OfficeQA145883120.8M1.1M
                                                                                                              DocVQA819593188.2M46.4M
                                                                                                              LiveMath154379123.2M3.6M
                                                                                                              ALFWorld5161,321259.3M15.9M

                                                                                                              Table 6 Cost and edit economy of the GPT-5.5 / GPT-5.5 (student / teacher) skill runs. Initial and final best_skill.md lengths are in tokens; Edits is the number of accepted bounded updates; Cost / pt is training tokens per absolute test-point gain. One representative learned rule per benchmark is shown in Figure 4. 表 6 展示 GPT-5.5 / GPT-5.5（学生 / 教师）技能运行的成本与编辑经济性。初始与最终的最佳 best_skill.md 长度以 token 计；Edits 表示已接受的有界更新次数；Cost / pt 为每获得一个绝对测试点增益所需的训练 token。每个基准各展示一条代表性的学习规则于图 4。

                                                                                                              4.4 Learned Skills: Compactness, Cost, and Examples

                                                                                                              4.4 学到的技能：紧凑性、成本与示例

                                                                                                              A central premise of SKILLOPT is that the trainable object should remain a small, inspectable text document. Tables 1-5 demonstrate that the optimizer is effective; this subsection asks what its output actually looks like and what it costs. We characterize the learned artifact on three axes-compactness, edit economy, and cost-per-point-and then show one representative learned rule per benchmark to illustrate what kind of procedural knowledge survives the bounded-update loop. SKILLOPT 的核心前提是：可训练对象应保持为一份小且可检查的文本文档。表 1-5 证明优化器是有效的；本小节将回答：它的输出究竟是什么样的，以及它要付出什么代价。我们从三个维度表征所学产物——紧凑性、编辑经济性以及单位点的成本——然后在每个基准上各展示一个代表性的学到规则，以说明在有界更新循环中，哪种类型的程序性知识仍会存活。

                                                                                                              Compactness. The final skills are uniformly small. Across the six benchmarks in Table 6, the final best_skill.md ranges from 379 tokens (LiveMathematicianBench) to 1,995 tokens (SpreadsheetBench), with a median of roughly 920 tokens. Even the longest learned skill is well below a typical system-prompt budget for modern frontier models, and the shortest one fits inside a single screen. The growth from initial to final skill is moderate (×2.5 to ×53 depending on whether the initial skill was a one-liner or a paragraph), but the final size in absolute tokens stays small enough that a domain practitioner can read, audit, and edit the deployed artifact in minutes. 紧凑性。最终技能整体都很小。表 6 中的六个基准上，最终最佳 best_skill.md 的规模从 379 tokens（LiveMathematicianBench）到 1,995 tokens（SpreadsheetBench），中位数约为 920 tokens。即使是最长的学到技能，也远低于现代前沿模型中典型的系统提示预算；最短的则可以完整放在一个屏幕内。从初始到最终技能的增长幅度适中 (×2.5 到 ×53（取决于初始技能是单行还是一段），但以绝对 tokens 计的最终大小仍小到足以让领域从业者在几分钟内阅读、审计并修改已部署的产物。

                                                                                                              Edit economy. A second striking pattern is that the gains come from very few accepted edits. Across the six benchmarks, the number of edits actually committed to best_skill.md during optimization is between 1 and 4 (median 2.5). LiveMathematicianBench's +29.3 point gain over no skill arises from a single accepted edit, and OfficeQA's +39.0 point gain similarly arises from one accepted edit. This is direct evidence that the validation gate is doing real work: the optimizer model proposes many more edits per epoch, but only a handful pass the held-out check and survive into the deployed skill. The bulk of the optimizer's text-space search is thus rejected, captured 编辑经济性。另一个显著的模式是：收益来自极少数被接受的编辑。在这六个基准上，优化过程中实际提交到 best_skill.md 的编辑次数介于 1 到 4 之间（中位数 2.5）。LiveMathematicianBench 相比“无技能”带来 +29.3 分的提升，来源于一次被接受的编辑；OfficeQA 的 +39.0 分提升同样来自一次被接受的编辑。这直接表明验证门确实在发挥作用：优化器模型在每个 epoch 中提出了更多编辑，但只有寥寥几个通过了所持出的检查并进入最终部署的技能。因此，优化器在文本空间中的大部分搜索都被否决了，

                                                                                                               

                                                                                                              SearchQA. "Infer the expected answer type from clue wording, then choose the shortest canonical entity SearchQA。 “根据线索措辞推断预期答案类型，然后选择最短的规范实体

                                                                                                              supported by co-occurring distinctive evidence." ，并以同时出现的独特证据予以支持。”

                                                                                                                  SpreadsheetBench. "Inspect workbook structure and formulas, then write evaluated static values across the full     SpreadsheetBench。 “检查工作簿结构与公式，然后在完整的

                                                                                                              requested target range instead of relying on Excel recalculation." 目标范围内写入已评估的静态数值，而不是依赖于 Excel 重新计算。”

                                                                                                                  OfficeQA. "Treat oracle parsed pages as primary evidence, lock table/date/unit context, and output exactly the     OfficeQA。 “将 oracle 解析得到的页面视为主要证据，锁定表格/日期/单位的上下文，并输出

                                                                                                                  requested rounded value without extra labels."     所要求的四舍五入数值本身，不要附加额外标签。”

                                                                                                                  DocVQA. "For tables, forms, charts, and legends, first bind the question to the exact visual row/header/field,     DocVQA。 “对于表格、表单、图表和图例，先把问题绑定到精确的可视行/表头/字段，

                                                                                                                  then copy only the aligned answer span."     然后只复制对齐的答案片段。”

                                                                                                                  LiveMathematicianBench. "In strongest-statement MCQs, rank choices by theorem strength and prefer a     LiveMathematicianBench。 “在最强表述的选择题中，按定理强度对选项排序，并优先选择一个

                                                                                                                  justified stronger-result option over true but weaker corollaries."     给出充分论证的更强结论选项，而不是虽然为真但更弱的推论。”

                                                                                                              ALFWorld. "Keep a horizon-aware visited/frontier ledger, diversify search after repeated same-type failures, ALFWorld。 “维护一个考虑地平线的已访问/前沿账本；在反复出现相同类型失败后分散搜索，

                                                                                                                  and avoid revisiting the destination until holding the target."     并在尚未掌握目标之前不要重新访问目的地。”

                                                                                                               

                                                                                                              Figure 4 Representative learned rules, one per benchmark, extracted from the final best_skill.md of the GPT-5.5 / GPT-5.5 runs in Table 6. Each rule is verbatim from the deployed skill. Notably, every rule is procedural rather than instance-specific, and several encode forms of discipline (answer formatting, evidence binding, search-frontier management) that frontier models do not apply zero-shot. 图4 每个基准提取的代表性已学规则一条，来自表6中 GPT-5.5 / GPT-5.5 运行的最终 best_skill.md。每条规则都与已部署技能逐字一致。值得注意的是，每条规则都是程序性的而非针对具体实例，并且包含多种纪律形式（答案格式、证据绑定、搜索前沿管理）；这些是前沿模型在零样本时不会应用的。

                                                                                                              by the rejected-edit buffer (Section 3.5) for future use, and never reaches the target model. The deployed skill is correspondingly compact rather than the union of every reflection. 由被拒绝的编辑缓冲区（第3.5节）供未来使用，并且永远达不到目标模型。相应地，已部署技能因此是紧凑的，而不是所有反思的并集。

                                                                                                              Cost per point of test-set gain. The training-token column quantifies the cost of operating the loop. Two regimes are visible. Procedural benchmarks where rollouts are short and cheap-SpreadsheetBench, OfficeQA, LiveMathematicianBench—reach 0.6-3.6M training tokens per absolute test-set point, even though the absolute gains on these benchmarks are the largest (e.g. +39.0 points on OfficeQA at 1.1M tokens / point, total 20.8M tokens). Benchmarks with longer trajectories or richer multimodal context—SearchQA (37.9M / pt) and DocVQA (46.4M / pt) - cost an order of magnitude more per point. The important deployment distinction is that this cost is paid once during skill training; after export, the optimized best_skill.md adds no optimizer calls, no weight updates, and only a compact text artifact to the target agent. 每个测试集增益点的成本。训练token列量化了运行该循环的代价。可以看到两种情形。程序性基准——运行很短且很便宜的 SpreadsheetBench、OfficeQA、LiveMathematicianBench——每个绝对测试集点达到0.6-3.6M训练token，即便这些基准上的绝对增益是最大的（例如：在OfficeQA上+39.0分，消耗1.1M tokens/点，总计20.8M tokens）。而在轨迹更长或多模态上下文更丰富的基准上——SearchQA（37.9M/pt）和DocVQA（46.4M/pt）——每点成本高出一个数量级。重要的部署差异在于：这笔成本只在技能训练阶段支付；导出之后，优化得到的best_skill.md不会再调用任何优化器、不会更新权重，只向目标智能体添加一个紧凑的文本产物。

                                                                                                              What does a learned skill actually say? Figure 4 reproduces one representative learned rule per benchmark, taken verbatim from the final best_skill.md of each case study in Table 6. Three observations stand out. First, the rules are procedural rather than instance-specific: none of them name a specific question, file, or entity. Second, they consistently encode the discipline that frontier models lack zero-shot: answer-format constraints (OfficeQA, LiveMathematicianBench), evidence binding to a specific visual region (DocVQA), workbook-structure-first reasoning (Spread-sheetBench), search-frontier discipline (ALFWorld), and canonical-entity choice (SearchQA). Third, they read like rules a thoughtful human practitioner would write after a day with the benchmark-except they are produced automatically by the optimizer and validated edit-by-edit on held-out data. 已学技能究竟说了什么？图4针对每个基准复现了一条代表性的已学规则，逐字取自表6中各案例研究的最终 best_skill.md。可以突出三点观察。第一，这些规则是程序性的而非针对具体实例：它们都没有点名某个特定问题、文件或实体。第二，它们持续编码前沿模型在零样本下缺乏的纪律：答案格式约束（OfficeQA、LiveMathematicianBench）、绑定到特定视觉区域的证据（DocVQA）、先以工作簿结构为核心的推理（Spread-sheetBench）、搜索前沿的纪律（ALFWorld）以及规范实体的选择（SearchQA）。第三，它们读起来像是一个深思熟虑的人类从业者在与该基准共度一天之后会写下的规则——不同之处在于：这些规则是由优化器自动生成的，并且在持出数据上逐次编辑验证。

                                                                                                              Implications. Together, the four observations above support a stronger version of the central claim. Compactness (<2,000 tokens) and edit economy (1-4 accepted edits) mean the deployed artifact is interpretable. Cost-per-point (0.6M-46.4M tokens / point) shows that the training cost is measurable and paid before deployment. The shape of the learned rules—procedural, generalizable, and consistent with what a thoughtful human practitioner would write - is evidence that text-space optimization with bounded updates and validation gating discovers transferable procedural knowledge rather than merely overfitting to the training split. This complements the cross-model, cross-harness, and cross-benchmark transfer evidence in Section 4.3: the artifact transfers because many of the rules it encodes are intrinsically transferable. 含义。综合上述四点观察，更强版本的核心论断得到支持。紧凑性（<2,000 tokens）和编辑经济性（1-4次被接受的编辑）意味着已部署的产物具有可解释性。按点计的成本（0.6M-46.4M tokens/点）表明训练成本是可度量的，并且在部署之前就已支付。已学规则的形态——程序性、可泛化性，并且与一个深思熟虑的人类从业者会写下的内容一致——证据显示：在受限更新与验证门控的条件下进行的文本空间优化，发现的是可迁移的程序性知识，而不只是对训练分割的过拟合。这与第4.3节中的跨模型、跨工具和跨基准迁移证据相互补充：因为它编码的许多规则本身就具有可迁移性，故该产物能够迁移。

                                                                                                              4.5 Qualitative Skill Evolution

                                                                                                              4.5 定性技能演化

                                                                                                              We inspect two representative runs to understand what the optimized skill actually learns. The ALFWorld case uses GPT-5.4-nano as the student and GPT-5.5 as the teacher, while the SpreadsheetBench case uses GPT-5.5 as both the frozen student and optimizer model. In both cases, SKILLOPT does not replace the initial skill with an unrelated prompt. Instead, accepted edits add compact procedural constraints around recurring failure modes observed in rollout trajectories. 我们检查两个代表性运行，以理解优化后的技能实际学到了什么。ALFWorld 案例使用 GPT-5.4-nano 作为学生、GPT-5.5 作为教师，而 SpreadsheetBench 案例则将 GPT-5.5 同时用作冻结学生和优化器模型。在这两种情况下，SKILLOPT 并不是用一个无关的提示来替换初始技能。相反，被接受的编辑会围绕回滚轨迹中观察到的重复失败模式，增加简洁的过程性约束。

                                                                                                              ALFWorld. The initial ALFWorld skill gives a generic household plan: search for the target object, pick it up, transform it if needed, and place it at the destination. The accepted edits make this plan more stateful and less loop-prone. The optimized skill learns exact object-name matching, so related objects such as mugs, cups, pans, and pots are not substituted for one another. It adds visited-location memory, so unvisited receptacles and surfaces are preferred over repeatedly checking likely but exhausted locations. It also adds destination memory, pick-two progress locks, and direct completion rules: once the agent can clean, heat, cool, place, or otherwise complete the next subgoal, it should take that admissible action instead of examining, closing, or verifying again. Qualitatively, the skill evolves from a general search-transform-place strategy into a finite-state execution policy with object identity, search memory, progress locks, and loop breakers. In this representative run, the selected skill improves ALFWorld held-out test performance from 49.3 to 74.6. ALFWorld。初始的 ALFWorld 技能给出的是一个通用的家务计划：搜索目标物体、将其拾起、在需要时进行变换，并把它放到目的地。被接受的编辑让这个计划更具状态性，也更不容易陷入循环。优化后的技能学会了精确的物体名称匹配，因此诸如 mug、cup、pan 和 pot 之类的相关物体不会彼此替代。它增加了已访问位置记忆，因此会优先查看未访问的容器和表面，而不是反复检查那些看似可能但已经耗尽的地点。它还增加了目的地记忆、双步进度锁以及直接完成规则：一旦智能体可以清洁、加热、冷却、放置，或以其他方式完成下一个子目标，就应采取该可接受动作，而不是再次检查、关闭或验证。定性地说，这个技能从通用的搜索-变换-放置策略演化为一种有限状态执行策略，包含物体身份、搜索记忆、进度锁和循环终止器。在这一代表性运行中，所选技能将 ALFWorld 留出测试性能从 49.3 提升到 74.6。

                                                                                                              SpreadsheetBench. The initial SpreadsheetBench skill already instructs the agent to use Python spreadsheet libraries and preserve unrelated workbook content. The accepted edits turn this generic automation workflow into a workbook-forensics policy. The optimized skill learns to inspect the actual workbook rather than rely on previews, locate headers and target ranges across multiple sheets, normalize keys and cell types before lookup or aggregation, and preserve formatting during structural edits. It also adds a key rule for formula-style prompts: when the grader reads cell values, the agent should compute and write evaluated static values, even if the prompt mentions formulas such as INDEX/MATCH or XLOOKUP. Later edits further require filling complete target ranges, including currently blank result cells, keeping helper computations in Python rather than adding workbook artifacts, and reopening the saved workbook to check boundary rows and remaining blanks. In this representative run, the selected skill improves SpreadsheetBench held-out test performance from 40.4 to 78.9. SpreadsheetBench。初始的 SpreadsheetBench 技能已经指示智能体使用 Python 电子表格库并保留无关的工作簿内容。被接受的编辑把这种通用自动化工作流变成了一种工作簿取证策略。优化后的技能学会了检查实际工作簿，而不是依赖预览，跨多个工作表定位表头和目标范围，在查找或聚合前规范化键和单元格类型，并在结构性编辑过程中保留格式。它还为公式式提示增加了一条关键规则：当评审器读取单元格值时，智能体应计算并写入求值后的静态值，即使提示中提到诸如 INDEX/MATCH 或 XLOOKUP 之类的公式。后续编辑进一步要求填满完整的目标范围，包括当前为空的结果单元格，将辅助计算保留在 Python 中而不是添加工作簿工件，并重新打开已保存的工作簿以检查边界行和剩余空白。在这一代表性运行中，所选技能将 SpreadsheetBench 留出测试性能从 40.4 提升到 78.9。

                                                                                                              5 Conclusion

                                                                                                              5 结论

                                                                                                              We presented SKILLOPT, a text-space optimizer that treats an external skill document as the trainable state for frozen LLM agents. By separating the target model that executes tasks from the optimizer that edits skills, and by using bounded edit budgets, minibatch reflection, held-out validation gates, rejected-edit buffers, and epoch-wise slow/meta update, SKILLOPT turns skill improvement into a controlled learning process rather than ad hoc prompt revision. Across six benchmarks, seven target models, and three execution modes, SKILLOPT is best or tied-best on 52 of 52 evaluated cells, lifts GPT-5.5 by +23.5 points on average over no skill in direct chat and by +24.8/ +19.1 points under Codex and Claude Code harnesses, and beats the strongest per-cell baseline from human, LLM, Trace2Skill, TextGrad, GEPA, and EvoSkill skills by +5.4 points on average. Per-benchmark case studies show that these gains arise from compact (<2,000 token), interpretable skill artifacts assembled from only 1-4 accepted edits, and that the deployed skills transfer across model scales, harnesses, and nearby benchmarks. These results suggest that compact natural-language skills can serve as a practical domain-adaptation layer for frontier agents, enabling reusable improvement without modifying model weights. 我们提出了 SKILLOPT：一种文本空间优化器，将外部技能文档视为冻结 LLM 代理的可训练状态。通过将执行任务的目标模型与编辑技能的优化器分离，并借助有界编辑预算、小批量反思、保留验证门、拒绝编辑缓冲区，以及按轮次的慢更新/元更新，SKILLOPT 把技能改进转化为受控的学习过程，而非随意的提示修订。在六个基准、七个目标模型和三种执行模式下，SKILLOPT 在 52/52 个评估单元中表现为最佳或并列最佳；相较于直接聊天中“不带技能”，它平均使 GPT-5.5 提升 +23.5 分，并在 Codex 与 Claude Code 的工具链中分别提升 +24.8/+19.1 分；同时，它以平均 +5.4 分的优势击败人类、LLM、Trace2Skill、TextGrad、GEPA 与 EvoSkill 中最强的逐单元基线。按基准的案例研究表明，这些增益来自紧凑的 (<2,000 token）、由仅 1-4 次被接受的编辑拼装出的可解释技能工件，并且已部署技能能够在模型规模、工具链以及相邻基准之间迁移。结果表明，紧凑的自然语言技能可以作为前沿代理的可行领域适配层，在不修改模型权重的情况下实现可复用的改进。

                                                                                                              Outlook. SKILLOPT optimizes a single skill artifact for a single target domain; natural extensions include skill libraries that share infrastructure across domains, reuse of optimizer-side meta skills across benchmarks, reward-free or preference-driven validation gates for open-ended tasks, and self-distillation of optimized skills back into the target model as a stepping stone toward weight-level adaptation. We hope that treating the skill itself as the trainable object—rather than as a side artifact of prompting-will let future work apply the full toolkit of optimization (learning rates, schedules, regularization, curricula, validation) to a part of the agent stack that has so far been hand-engineered. 展望。SKILLOPT 为单个目标领域优化单一技能工件；自然的扩展包括构建跨领域共享基础设施的技能库、在不同基准间复用优化器侧的元技能、针对开放式任务采用无奖励或基于偏好的验证门，以及将优化后的技能再蒸馏回目标模型，作为迈向权重级适配的垫脚石。我们希望，把“技能本身”视为可训练对象——而非把它当作提示的附属产物——将让未来工作把优化的完整工具箱（学习率、调度、正则化、课程、验证）应用到目前仍主要依赖人工工程的代理栈部分。

Omni-math: A universal olympiad level mathematic benchmark for large language models, 2024. URL https://arxiv.org/abs/2410.07985. [14] Bofei Gao, Feifan Song, Zhe Yang, Zefan Cai, Yibo Miao, Qingxiu Dong, Lei Li, Chenghao Ma, Liang Chen, Runxin Xu, Zhengyang Tang, Benyou Wang, Daoguang Zan, Shanghaoran Quan, Ge Zhang, Lei Sha, Yichang Zhang, Xuancheng Ren, Tianyu Liu, 和 Baobao Chang.
